claims.text: The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) variant is the most common mutation in this Malaysian IOPD cohort, occurring in 8 patients (10 alleles) with an allele frequency of 33%.

citations.quote: The most common mutation identified was c.1935C>A p.(D645E), occurring in eight patients (10 alleles) with allele frequency of (33%).

claims.text: Two unrelated patients were homozygous for c.1935C>A p.(D645E), and the remaining patients carrying this variant were compound heterozygotes.

citations.quote: Two unrelated patients were homozygous for the c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation. The other 13 were compound heterozygotes.

claims.text: Patient 13 (Chinese, homozygous c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) had GAA activity 2.0%, age at presentation 4 months, failure to thrive, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, hepatomegaly, CK 1100 U/L, cardiomegaly, EF 28%, LVMI 483 g/m2, heart failure; deceased at age 2 years.

Patient 17 (Malay, homozygous c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) had GAA activity 1.7%, age at presentation 2 months, failure to thrive, all classic IOPD features (cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, hepatomegaly), CK 930 U/L, cardiomegaly, EF 29%, heart failure; deceased at age 6 months.

Patient 5 (Chinese, compound heterozygous c.2024_2026delACA p.(N675del) / c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) had all classic IOPD features, age at presentation 4 months, failure to thrive, heart failure; ERT started at 7 months, deceased at 8 months.

Patient 6 (Chinese, compound heterozygous c.1843G>A p.(G615R) / c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) had all classic IOPD features, age at presentation 6 months, failure to thrive, heart failure; deceased at 10 months.

Patient 7 (Chinese, compound heterozygous c.1411_1414delGAGA p.(E471Pfs*5) / c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) had GAA activity <0.1†, age at presentation 3 months, classic IOPD features (cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties), CK NA, EF 9.9%, LVMI 485 g/m2, heart failure; deceased at 2 years.

Patient 9 (Chinese, compound heterozygous c.1411_1414delGAGA p.(E471Pfs*5) / c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) had all classic IOPD features, age at presentation 3 months, failure to thrive, heart failure; deceased at 3 years.

Patient 12 (Chinese, compound heterozygous c.2024_2026delACA p.(N675del) / c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) had GAA activity 5.3%, age at presentation 2 months, classic IOPD features, CK 909, EF 81.6%, LVMI 159 g/m2, no heart failure; ERT started at age 2 months, alive at 5 years 10 months.

Patient 15 (Chinese, compound heterozygous c.2662G>T p.(E888*) / c.1935C>A p.(D645E)) had GAA activity 0.6%, age at presentation 4 months, all classic IOPD features, CK 847, EF 33%, LVMI 461 g/m2, heart failure; deceased at 7 months.
The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation has been reported to cause both IOPD and LOPD, and is the most common mutation in Southern China (25%), Taiwan (36%) and Thailand (32.4%).

citations.quote: This mutation has been reported to cause both IOPD and LOPD [28, 29]. The Chinese patients in Malaysia share other common mutations with patients from China and Taiwan

claims.text: The most frequently identified mutation in the cohort is c.1935C>A p.(D645E), with an allele frequency of 33%, consistent with reports from Southern China, Taiwan, and Thailand.

citations.quote: The most frequently identified mutation in our cohort is c.1935C>A p.(D645E), with an allele frequency of 33%. This has also been reported to be the most common mutation in Southern China (25%), Taiwan (36%) and Thailand (32.4%)

claims.text: Four patients in the cohort share the molecular mutations homozygous c.1935C>A p.(D645E) or compound heterozygous c.1411_1414del p.(E471Pfs*5) / c.1935C>A p.(D645E); all four deceased between ages 6 months and 3 years without achieving any motor milestone, contrasting with Taiwanese newborn-screened patients with the same mutations who achieved independent walking.

citations.quote: Four of our patients share similar molecular mutations [two patients were homozygous for c.1935C>A p.(D645E), two patients were compound heterozygous for c.1411_1414del p.(E471Pfs*5) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E)] with patients in a study in Taiwan who were diagnosed via newborn screening and given very early ERT prior to symptom onset

claims.text: The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) variant is described as a mutation (implicitly classified as disease-causing/pathogenic) in the context of IOPD; its CRIM status for affected patients is predicted as Positive from the Erasmus Pompe database.

citations.quote: CRIM status predicted based on Erasmus Pompe database (http://www.pompevariantdatabase.nl/ updated in Dec 2020)

claims.text: The cohort's presenting features included hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (100%), respiratory insufficiency (94%), hypotonia (88%), failure to thrive (82%), feeding difficulties (76%), and hepatomegaly (76%), with a median age at presentation of 3 months and median age of diagnosis of 6 months.

citations.quote: Clinical features documented at presentation were hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (100%), respiratory weakness (94%), hypotonia (88%), failure to thrive (82%), feeding difficulties (76%) and hepatomegaly (76%). Our cohort had a median age at presentation of 3 months. The median age of diagnosis was 6 months
